items.title: Afgang van jewelste: Qarabag na 45 minuten met 0-5 achter in de Champions League
items.summary: Qarabag FK krijgt in de tussenronde van de Champions League alle hoeken van het veld te zien. De club uit Azerbeidzjan stond na 45 minuten spelen al op een 0-5 achterstand tegen Newcastle United.

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Gordon makes UCL history with 4 goals in 1st half
items.summary: Anthony Gordon became just the second player to score four goals in the first half of a UEFA Champions League game and the first to do it in the knockout round.

items.title: Balogun double sets U.S. mark in UCL knockouts
items.summary: American forward Folarin Balogun took less than one minute to give Monaco the lead against Paris Saint-Germain in their Champions League playoff on Tuesday.

items.title: Van Dijk backs Szoboszlai as future L'pool leader
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk believes midfielder Dominik Szoboszlai has all the qualities to one day succeed him as Liverpool captain having led by example this season.

items.title: Coutinho laat contract in Brazilië ontbinden: 'Ik ben mentaal moe'
items.summary: Het avontuur van Philippe Coutinho bij Vasco da Gama is ten einde. De oud-speler van Liverpool en Barcelona keerde medio 2024 terug op het oude nest, maar heeft wegens mentale redenen zijn contract laten ontbinden.

items.title: Qarabag v Newcastle United: Champions League playoff – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League playoff first leg, 5.45pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Xaymaca 7 min: Newcastle are carving Qarabag open. Joe Willock runs through the middle before slipping in Harvey Barnes. Barnes hits it down to the keeper’s left and it’s out for a corner. 5 min: Qarabag head the other way, working it well on the left. It rolls out to Joni Montiel but his effort is blocked and goes out for a corner. Continue reading...

items.title: Vinícius, Mourinho and treating racism as reputational risk rather than a lived reality | Jonathan Liew
items.summary: The Brazilian has seen this before, football has seen this before and yet why does it feel like nothing ever changes? José Mourinho: against provoking opposition fans. José Mourinho: in favour of restrained celebrations. José Mourinho, once of the poke-in-the-eye, sprint-down-the-touchline, accost-the-referee-in-the-car-park school of footballing expression: now apparently very big on showing respect to the game. Well, it seems like we’ve all been on a journey here. “I told him the biggest person in the history of this club was Black,” Mourinho recounted when asked about his conversation with Vinícius Júnior on Tuesday night. “This club, the last thing that it is, is racist.” And doubtless these words will have been a profound source of comfort to Vinícius in his lowest moment, having been insulted on the pitch by an opposition player in a Champions League playoff. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Anthony Gordon maakt er vier in één helft tegen Qarabag
items.summary: Woensdagavond staan er weer vier wedstrijden in de tussenronde van de Champions League op het programma. Onder meer Newcastle United, Internazionale en Atlético Madrid komen in actie. Daarnaast spelen Arsenal en AC Milan competitieduels. Volg het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Vítor Pereira back on familiar ground as he begins Nottingham Forest revival mission
items.summary: Portuguese managed Fenerbahce and leads his new side into their Europa League playoff sounding confident As Vítor Pereira wrapped up his pre-match media duties at Sukru Saracoglu Stadium on Wednesday evening, his assistant Luís Miguel Moreira da Silva waited at the mouth of the tunnel. “Let’s go?” he said as Pereira eventually emerged, before the Nottingham Forest squad followed the pair on to the pitch. Then it was down to business, Pereira’s first assignment in charge of Forest at one of his 13 former clubs, Fenerbahce. For Pereira, the Kadikoy district of Istanbul represents familiar territory, having lived in the city across two enjoyable but trophy-less spells here as a manager, most recently in 2021. Continue reading...

items.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Arsenal: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott The preamble from the reverse fixture two months ago bears repeating. Because, I mean, c’mon … … yet while this will be the 42nd 43rd time the Premier League leaders have played the side propping up the entire table - winning 30 out of 41 31 out of 42, with seven draws – who are responsible for two of the four historical shocks? Why, Wolverhampton Wanderers, that’s who! Click below to reminisce, my old MBM pals. So while Mikel Arteta will surely be anticipating another three points, Rob Edwards must know that long shots sometimes find the target, and you never know. They nearly pulled off a shock at the Emirates back in December, after all. Kick-off is at 8pm GMT. It’s on! Continue reading...

items.title: Mijnans: 'Mooi om met AZ op drie fronten voor de prijzen te spelen'
items.summary: Sven Mijnans (25) staat donderdag voor zijn 36ste Europese wedstrijd voor AZ. De aanvoerder hoopt dit seizoen hoge ogen te gaan gooien in de Conference League. Samen met de Alkmaarders wil de middenvelder tot de laatste dag meedoen om de prijzen.

items.title: Franse media: opvolger van De Zerbi bij Marseille bekend
items.summary: Olympique Marseille heeft volgens Franse media, een week na het ontslag van Roberto De Zerbi , een akkoord bereikt met een nieuwe trainer. De Senegalees Habib Beye moet iets aan de wisselvallige resultaten van de ploeg van Quinten Timber en Jeffrey De Lange doen.

items.title: Vinicius: Eight years at Real Madrid, 20 cases of alleged racist abuse
items.summary: Spanish football expert Guillem Balague details how Vincius Jr has become a global symbol of resistance against discrimination.

items.title: Fifa’s plan for expanded 48-team Club World Cup will not be blocked by Uefa
items.summary: Backing a sign of improved relations between presidents Tournament expected not to be held every two years Uefa is ready to back Fifa’s proposed expansion of the Club World Cup to 48 teams for the next edition in 2029 in a sign of improving relations between their respective presidents, Aleksander Ceferin and Gianni Infantino. The European football governing body had opposed plans to grow the Club World Cup over concerns an expanded tournament could threaten the status of the Champions League, but Uefa is now willing to back Fifa in return for an undertaking that the competition will not be held every two years. Continue reading...

items.title: What is Uefa's racism protocol and is it working?
items.summary: Rather than player welfare, the focus was about racism protocol and process. Did this fail Real Madrid's Vinicius Jr against Benfica?

items.title: Who's done the fastest times and most laps at F1's final pre-season test?
items.summary: Find out who has the fastest times and which teams have completed the most laps at the final F1 pre-season test in Bahrain so far.

items.title: Rock carrying chalk after blaming Belgian soap for Premier League struggles
items.summary: Josh Rock is ready to wash his hands of Belgian soap after blaming last week's Premier League hammering in Antwerp on a pre-match visit to the toilet.

items.title: Van Gerwen a major doubt for Premier League Darts in Glasgow with 'medical issue'
items.summary: Michael van Gerwen is a major doubt to take part in Thursday night's Premier League Darts in Glasgow - live on Sky Sports - due to a 'medical issue'.

items.title: Make or break? Lose playoff and Simeone's Atlético reign could be over
items.summary: Atlético Madrid have stunk the house out in 2026. Can Diego Simeone, the club's long-serving and successful coach, work his magic? Or is it time to go?
